text.title: AI-in-PM Agent Patterns and Gotchas
text.summary: Decision-support and execution agent patterns, risk report prompt, bottleneck detection, DORA metrics script, and PMBOK 8 compliance gotchas.

rule.statement: Configure agents for decision support, not autonomous PM action: agents surface risk assessments, status summaries, and bottleneck alerts for human PM review — they do not reassign resources, close issues, or change deadlines unilaterally.
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 requires human oversight for AI-assisted decisions; autonomous PM actions by agents without approval violates the ethical AI standard and removes accountability for outcomes.

rule.statement: Label all AI-generated risk scores as "AI-estimated" in output and audit log — require human PM validation before acting on them.
rule.rationale: AI risk assessments produce confident-sounding probability scores even when data is insufficient; the "AI-estimated" label prevents risk scores from being treated as validated measurements.

section.title: Risk Report Prompt
section.reference: XML prompt for project health report with RAG status and risk assessment

example.code: <task>Generate a project health report with risk assessment.</task> <project_data> <open_issues>{{count}}</open_issues> <overdue_tasks>{{list}}</overdue_tasks> <blockers>{{list}}</blockers> <team_capacity>{{available_hours_this_week}}</team_capacity> <deadline>{{target_date}}</deadline> </project_data> <output> 1. RAG status (Red/Amber/Green) with justification 2. Top 3 risks with probability (High/Medium/Low) and impact 3. Recommended mitigation for each risk 4. Flag: any risks beyond human PM authority to resolve? </output> <constraint>Mark all AI-generated risk scores as "AI-estimated" for audit log purposes.</constraint>
example.note: Explicit AI-estimated labeling constraint prevents risk scores from being treated as validated measurements in downstream reporting.

section.title: Bottleneck Detection

rule.statement: To detect the DORA AI productivity paradox: compare individual task completion rate vs. deployment frequency over the same period. If task rate rises while deployment frequency is flat, trace where work accumulates (review queue depth, staging backlog, deployment frequency).
rule.rationale: Bottleneck detection requires comparing two different metric types simultaneously; looking at only one metric produces the illusion of improvement while the real constraint stays invisible.

section.reference: Bash script to count deployments from GitHub releases

rule.statement: Agents cannot detect team morale, interpersonal conflicts, or burnout from project data — these are critical PM risk factors that only human observation can identify.
rule.rationale: Project health data (issue counts, velocity, cycle time) has no signal for human factors; treating data-driven AI health reports as complete PM risk assessments omits the most common causes of project failure.

antipattern.description: Using AI status reports as the sole input for executive project health briefings.
antipattern.reason: AI reports can be accurate on measurable metrics but contextually misleading without narrative that explains anomalies, team dynamics, and qualitative risk factors that only humans can observe.
